Order MultipleThe applied order multiple. Example: step 5 → 5, 10, 15 …
Estimated CBMCBM volume required to load the recommended quantity (m³).

Recommendation Basis (4 lines)

① Target Stock

Daily avg × target days = the target inventory to secure

② Projected Stock

Current stock + expected inbound + back order impact

③ Actual Need

Target stock − projected stock, rounded up (ROUNDUP) to the order multiple

④ Velocity Context

7D vs. 30D average comparison and rate of change (%). Indicates whether recent sales are accelerating or decelerating.

What-If Simulation

InputDescription
Recommended Order QuantityEnter the quantity you intend to order. Adjust in order multiple increments. Initial value is the recommended quantity.
Hypothetical ETASet the expected arrival date with a date picker. Initial value is 90 days from today.
Reset to RecommendedResets inputs to the system's recommended quantity and default ETA.

Simulation Results (5 figures)

Demand Before ETA

Expected sales volume before arrival (daily avg × remaining days).

Back Order Before ETA

Back order if stock is depleted before ETA. Highlighted in warning color when greater than 0.

Back Order After ETA

Remaining back order after arrival. Greater than 0 still means a stock shortage.

Required CBM

CBM needed to load the entered order quantity.

Projected SOD

Estimated sell-out date under the simulation conditions.

Comparison Line: Below the simulation results, three figures appear side by side: Current SOD | Simulation SOD | Remaining after ETA — so you can immediately see the improvement over the current situation.

Calculation Detail (4-column grid)

ItemDescription
Target DaysTarget inventory days set in the top controls
Daily Avg SalesCurrent daily average sales velocity
Target Stock= Daily avg × target days
Current StockTotal current warehouse inventory
Inbound / Inbound (incl. Draft)Total expected inbound (Draft inclusion labeled)
Back Order ImpactCurrent back order quantity (negative reduces projected stock)
Projected Stock= Current stock + inbound + back order impact
Required Qty= Target stock − projected stock (raw need before rounding)
✅
MOQ & Order Multiple Auto-Applied: If the required quantity is less than MOQ, MOQ is applied automatically. The final recommended quantity is always rounded up (ROUNDUP) to the nearest order multiple. MOQ and order multiples must be set correctly in SKU Master.

⑦ Demand Forecast Tab

▲ Demand Forecast Tab — Forward mode: Actual demand (blue) · Forecast (orange dashed) · Stockout line (fuchsia dashed) · Meta strip

Uses AI to predict how many units of this SKU will sell each week in the future. Use the forecast to see when stock might run out and plan your orders before that happens.

💡
The tab has two modes: Forward (predict future sales from today) and Backtest (check how accurate the model was against real past sales).

Forward Mode — Future Forecast

The chart displays four elements. You can drag to pan left/right and scroll to zoom in or out.

Chart ElementColorDescription
Actual demandBlue solid lineHistorical weekly sales data
ForecastOrange solid lineModel-predicted future weekly sales
Forecast rangeOrange shaded bandThe range where actual sales are expected to land. Default P85 means about 85% of weeks should fall inside this band. A wider band means the forecast is less certain.
Stockout lineFuchsia (#C026D3) dashed lineThe estimated date when current inventory will be fully consumed at the forecast sales rate. An order must arrive before this date to avoid a stockout. Not shown if stock is sufficient through the forecast period.

Forward Mode Controls

ControlDescription
Forecast weeksHow many weeks to forecast (1–52). Default 13 weeks. Exceeding the stored horizon triggers a server re-run (5–30 seconds). Apply with Enter or clicking outside the input.
HistoryHow much historical data to show in the chart. Select a calendar date or enter a week count. Reducing history does not delete data — it only changes the initial chart zoom. Scroll left to view older history.
ModelForecast model selection. Default Auto picks the best model for this SKU automatically. Changing the model triggers a server re-run (5–30 seconds).

Forward Mode — Meta Strip

Click any item to see a detailed explanation popup.

ItemDescription
Order RangeThe date range of sales data used to train this forecast model
Forecasted StockoutEstimated date inventory hits zero. "None" means stock is sufficient through the forecast period.
ModelModel name actually used (Auto resolves to a specific model name)
ConfidenceThe confidence level of the forecast range (default P85)
Forecast runDate and time this forecast was last computed

Backtest Mode — Accuracy Validation

▲ Demand Forecast Tab — Backtest mode: PXX interval · Forecast vs. actual · Accuracy metrics (MAE · WAPE · MASE · PXX Coverage)
💡
What is a backtest? It answers: "If I had used this model 6 months ago, how close would the forecasts have been to real sales?" This tells you whether to trust the model for this SKU.
ControlDescription
CutoffThe forecast start point. Data before this date is used for training; actual sales after it are compared against the forecast. Must be a Monday.
HorizonHow many weeks ahead to forecast from the cutoff (1–52)
TrainLimit training data to the most recent N weeks. 0 uses all available history.
HistoryHow much pre-cutoff data to show in the chart. Zoom only — scroll left to see older data.
Model 1Primary forecast model
Model 2 (Ensemble)Optional second model. When selected, the average of both models is plotted as an additional line.
RunExecute the backtest with the current settings. First run may take 5–30 seconds.

Backtest Results — Accuracy Metrics

Click any metric to see a detailed explanation popup.

MetricDescriptionBenchmark
MAEThe average gap between what was forecast and what actually sold, in units.Lower is better
WAPEHow far off the forecast was, shown as a percentage. Lower means more accurate.≤10% excellent · ≤20% acceptable
MASEHow much better the model is vs. just repeating last week's sales. Below 1.0 means the AI forecast is better than that simple approach.<1.0 = useful model
PXX CoverageHow often actual sales landed inside the forecast band (Low to High). The label updates with the selected Confidence level — e.g. P85 Coverage means the band should capture ~85% of weeks.~PXX% is ideal
ModelModel name used—
Train / EvalWeeks used for training / weeks used for testing—
Horizon actualTotal units actually sold during the test period—
Horizon forecastTotal units the model predicted for the test period—
DifferenceForecast total minus actual total. Positive = forecast was too high, negative = forecast was too low.Closer to 0 is better

Forecast Model Guide

Use Auto unless you have a specific reason to choose otherwise.

🤖 Auto (Recommended)

Tests all models against past sales and picks the one with the smallest error. Use this unless you have a specific reason not to.

📊 AutoARIMA

Good for products that follow a seasonal pattern — selling more at certain times of year.

📈 AutoETS

Good for products whose sales are gradually trending up or down.

📉 AutoTheta

Good for products that are steadily growing or declining over a long period.

📋 Window Avg

Uses the average of the most recent N weeks. Works well when sales are steady with no clear trend.

📋 Historic Avg

Uses the average of all past sales. Best when sales have stayed roughly the same for a long time.

🔄 Seasonal Naïve

Copies last year's sales for the same week. Best when the same seasonal pattern repeats every year.

➡️ Naïve

Repeats last week's number. Used for very erratic products or as a comparison baseline.

⚠️
Forecast server required: The Demand Forecast tab only works when the forecast server (FastAPI) is running. If no chart appears, contact your administrator.
en-demand-forecast-page:
📈
Menu › Planning › Demand Forecast

Demand Forecast Page

A dedicated page that classifies all active SKUs by segment and gives a high-level view of forecast model coverage and performance.

② Segmentation Overview

The top of the page summarises all active SKUs across three segments. Every time the forecast pipeline runs, each SKU's sales history is re-evaluated and its segment assignment is updated automatically.

How SKUs are classified

The system looks at two things from each SKU's sales history:

  • Weeks with no sales: What share of all weeks had zero units sold
  • Average weekly sales: How many units typically sell per week
ConditionSegment
Zero-sale rate ≥ 30%, or weekly mean < 3Intermittent
Everything else — consistent, low-variance demandSmooth

Smooth SKUs are then split by history length:

Active historySub-segment
Under 52 weeks — less than one full annual cycleSmooth / Short History
52 weeks or more — at least one full annual cycleSmooth / Full History
🔄
Recent activity counts: The last 13 weeks are checked separately. If an Intermittent SKU has started selling regularly in recent weeks, it gets promoted to Smooth / Short History. If a Smooth SKU has barely sold anything recently, it gets moved down to Intermittent.

Metric Cards (4)

CardDescription
Total SKUsTotal number of active SKUs registered in the system
Forecasted SKUsNumber of SKUs with an active forecast model applied, and their share of the total
Demand CoveragePercentage of total demand accounted for by forecasted SKUs
N-Week DemandTotal units sold in the selected lookback window, with the date range shown

Filter Controls

ControlDescription
Lookback windowPreset buttons (4W · 8W · 10W · 13W · 26W · 52W) or free-text custom input (1–104 weeks). Applies to metric cards and the segment table simultaneously.
Product typeToggle Car Cover / Seat Cover / Floor Mat individually or select All. Affects the segment table and concentration graph.

Segment Table

A three-row table showing the key metrics for each segment. Click any row to navigate to that segment's detail page.

ColumnDescription
SegmentSmooth / Smooth Short History / Intermittent. Hover the ? icon for a description of each segment.
MethodThe forecasting method badge for each segment (StatsForecast · V1 · Restock policy). Click the badge to open a detailed model explanation dialog.
SKUNumber of SKUs in this segment
N-Week DemandTotal units sold in the selected window for this segment
% of DemandThis segment's share of total demand, shown as a bar
▲ Segmentation Overview — four metric cards and the segment table showing SKU counts and demand share

① Run Forecast

The Run Forecast card at the top of the page lets you trigger the full forecast pipeline on demand. The card header shows the last run date/time and horizon.

Horizon setting

Choose a preset (4W · 8W · 10W · 13W · 26W · 52W) or type a custom value (1–104 weeks), then click Run.

Progress and output

While running, a 7-step progress bar tracks the pipeline:

StepWhat happens
IngestPull latest data from external sources
LoadLoad data into the pipeline
ProfileClassify each SKU into its segment
BacktestTest each model against past sales data
SelectPick the best model per SKU by WAPE
ForecastGenerate forward forecasts
WritePersist results to the database

A collapsible Output panel shows the live log stream. Click Cancel at any time to stop the run — no data is written on cancellation. If you navigate away mid-run, the job continues and re-attaches automatically when you return to the page.

▲ Run Forecast card — 7-step progress bar, live log output, and completion status

③ Demand Concentration Graph

A Pareto curve showing how cumulative demand builds as SKUs are ranked from highest to lowest volume. The further the curve bows above the diagonal (even distribution line), the more demand is concentrated in a small number of SKUs.

  • Fixed annotation: Shows the overall share of demand covered by all forecasted SKUs.
  • Hover annotation: Move your cursor over the chart to see the exact SKU% → demand% at any point on the curve.
💡
A highly concentrated curve means that forecast accuracy on a small number of top SKUs has an outsized impact on overall planning quality.
▲ Demand Concentration — cumulative SKU demand Pareto curve with forecasted coverage annotation

④ Smooth / Full History Segment Detail

SKUs with 52 or more weeks of stable, regular sales. The pipeline runs multiple candidate models and automatically selects the best one per SKU.

Candidate models and selection

Six candidate models are tested for each SKU:

ModelHow it works
AutoARIMALearns both the overall sales trend and seasonal patterns (e.g. selling more in summer).
AutoETS (damped)Puts more weight on recent sales, while assuming the trend will eventually level off.
AutoThetaSplits the sales data into trend and seasonal parts, forecasts each separately, then adds them together.
Window Average (8 weeks)Uses the average of the last 8 weeks as the forecast.
NaiveAssumes next week will be the same as last week.
Historic AverageUses the average of all past sales. Works best when sales have been consistent over a long time.

Each model is tested against real past sales to see how close its predictions would have been. The model with the smallest error rate is automatically chosen for each SKU.

Confidence scoring

How accurate the model was during testing (shown as Train WAPE) determines its confidence label:

Train WAPEConfidence
≤ 35%standard
> 35%low (red badge)

Mode Tabs

ModeDescription
ForwardShows the latest forecast results. Compares upcoming N-week forecasts against recent actual demand.
BacktestSelect a completed past forecast run and evaluate forecast vs. actual demand. Accuracy cards (WAPE, Bias, PXX Coverage) are shown above the table.
Use test dataEnable the Use test data checkbox above the backtest cycle list to explore backtest functionality using built-in sample data instead of real forecast runs. Useful when no live forecast has been executed yet.
SimulationRun a what-if forecast directly from the UI. Choose a cutoff date, horizon, and model, then click Run.

Table Columns

ColumnShown inDescription
SKUAll modesClick to navigate to the SKU forecast detail page.
ModelAll modesThe forecast model selected for this SKU
ConfidenceForward onlystandard or low (red badge). Hover the ? icon in the column header to see what low confidence means.
Train WAPEForward onlyHow far off the model was when tested against past sales, as a percentage. Lower is better. Shown in red when confidence is low.
Weeks of historyAll modesLength of available sales history for this SKU in weeks
N-Week Demand / ActualAll modesActual units sold in the selected period
N-Week Forecast / ForecastAll modesForecasted units. When a confidence interval is available, hover to see Low · Point · High values in a tooltip.
WAPEBacktest · SimulationPer-SKU forecast error rate (%).
V1 WAPEBacktest · Simulation (toggle)Per-SKU error rate using the V1 rule-based formula. Enable via the Columns button.
Diff (model−V1)Backtest · Simulation (toggle)Model WAPE − V1 WAPE. Negative (green) means the model beats V1; positive (red) means V1 is better. Enable via Columns.

Column Visibility (Columns button) — Smooth / Full History only

The Columns button appears only for the Smooth / Full History segment. The short history segment does not have confidence intervals or V1 comparison, so the button is not shown there.

ColumnDefaultAvailable inDescription
LowOffAll modesLower bound of the forecast interval. Changes with the Confidence level selector (default P85 → 15th percentile).
MidpointOnAll modesPoint forecast. When Low or High are also shown, the interval hover tooltip is hidden and only the number is displayed.
HighOffAll modesUpper bound of the forecast interval. Changes with the Confidence level selector (default P85 → 85th percentile).
V1 WAPEOffBacktest · SimulationPer-SKU error rate from the V1 formula. Enabling this also shows V1 Comparison Cards above the table.
Diff (model−V1)OffBacktest · SimulationModel WAPE minus V1 WAPE. Negative = model better. Enabling this also shows V1 Comparison Cards.

Confidence Level Selector

The Confidence level selector above the table controls the width of the prediction interval. The selected level is reflected in the Low and High columns, the hover tooltip, and the Coverage metric in the Backtest accuracy cards.

LabelInterval widthP(demand ≤ High)Use case
P70Central 40%70%Optimistic / minimize overstock
P80Central 60%80%Balanced planning
P85Central 70%85%Default — recommended for standard inventory planning
P90Central 80%90%Conservative / minimize stockout risk
P95Central 90%95%High-risk SKUs / maximum safety stock
💡
The PXX number is the probability that actual demand falls at or below the High value. Selecting P90 means "9 out of 10 times, actual sales will be at or below the High bound." Higher levels produce wider intervals and are better suited for conservative planning.

Sorting and Search

  • Click any column header to sort ascending / descending.
  • Shift+click additional column headers to sort by multiple columns simultaneously. Priority numbers appear on each sort icon.
  • Use the search box to filter by SKU ID in real time. Page size options: 50 · 100 · 200 rows.
  • Column headers remain sticky at the top while scrolling.
▲ Smooth / Full History — Forward mode table (model, confidence, Train WAPE, and forecast columns)

Backtest Mode — Accuracy Cards

MetricDescription
WAPEWeighted absolute error across all SKUs with actual demand. Green <20%, amber 20–40%, red ≥40%.
BiasOver- or under-forecast direction. Green when absolute bias is under 10%.
PXX CoveragePercentage of SKUs where actual demand fell within the [Low, High] interval. The label updates to match the selected Confidence level. Green ≥65% (for P85 default).

Backtest / Simulation — V1 Comparison Cards

When at least one V1 column (V1 WAPE or Diff) is enabled via the Columns button, three V1 Comparison Cards appear below the accuracy cards. V1 is the previous rule-based forecast method. These cards let you see whether the AI model is more accurate than V1.

CardDescription
V1 WAPEV1's error rate (%), using the same color scale as the AI model (green <20%, amber 20–40%, red ≥40%).
MASE vs V1Compares total forecast errors between AI and V1. Below 1.0 (green) = AI made fewer errors; above 1.0 (red) = V1 was more accurate.
WAPE Diff (V1 − Model)V1's error rate minus the AI model's error rate. Positive (green) = AI wins; negative (red) = V1 wins.
💡
V1 comparison is available only for the Smooth / Full History segment. SKUs without V1 data are excluded from the card calculations.

Simulation Mode

SettingDescription
Cutoff dateOnly data up to this date is used to train the model. Default: 13 weeks before today.
Horizon (weeks)How many weeks to forecast forward from the cutoff date.
ModelAuto (pipeline selection) or a specific model: AutoETS · AutoARIMA · AutoTheta · Window Avg · Historic Avg · Seasonal Naïve · Naïve

A progress bar shows each step as it runs. You can cancel at any time. After the simulation finishes, enable V1 columns in the Columns button to compare the simulation results against V1.

▲ Simulation mode — cutoff date, horizon, and model controls with the resulting forecast table

⑤ Smooth / Short History Segment Detail

SKUs with steady sales but fewer than 52 weeks of history. Without a full year of data, it's hard to tell real seasonal patterns from random variation, so only two simple models are compared.

Candidate models

ModelHow it works
AutoETS (non-seasonal)Tracks the general level and trend of sales, without trying to find seasonal patterns. Safer when there isn't enough data to confirm seasonality.
Window Average (12 weeks)Uses the average of the last 12 weeks as the forecast.

Whichever model had the smaller error in testing is chosen. Because there isn't much history yet, all short-history SKUs are automatically treated as low confidence — the Confidence badge and Train WAPE column are not shown.

Additional ColumnDescription
Weeks to full historyWeeks remaining until this SKU is reclassified into the Smooth / Full History segment. Highlighted green when ≤ 8 weeks away; shows a Promoted badge when already at the threshold.

Default sort is Weeks to full history ascending — SKUs closest to graduation appear first.

▲ Smooth / Short History — Forward mode table with Weeks to full history column highlighted

Backtest Mode

The short history segment also supports Backtest mode. Select a past forecast run to compare forecast vs. actual demand — the same WAPE, Bias, and PXX Coverage accuracy cards appear above the table. If no real forecast runs exist yet, enable the Use test data checkbox to try the feature with built-in smooth/short sample SKUs.

▲ Short History Backtest mode — accuracy cards (WAPE · Bias · PXX Coverage) and forecast vs. actual comparison table

⑥ Intermittent Segment Detail

SKUs that go weeks without any sales, or sell fewer than 3 units per week on average. Because sales are so irregular, standard forecast methods don't work well here — the system uses special methods designed for infrequent demand.

Forecasting methodology

ModelHow it works
Croston OptimizedSeparately estimates how often this SKU sells and how many units sell each time, then combines both into a forecast.
IMAPALooks at sales data across different time spans (weekly, monthly, etc.) at the same time and averages the results. Works better for very irregular SKUs.

Because sales are so infrequent, it's hard to reliably measure forecast accuracy. Instead of accuracy scores, the table shows inventory signals to help you decide whether to keep ordering this SKU — like when it last sold and how often it typically sells.

ColumnDescription
SKUClick to navigate to the SKU forecast detail page.
N-Week UnitsUnits sold in the selected lookback window
Last sale dateThe week date of the most recent sale
Weeks since last saleWeeks elapsed since the last sale. Amber when >13 weeks, red when >26 weeks.
Event countTotal number of weeks in which any sale occurred (across all history)
Avg units / eventAverage units sold per sale week
💡
Last updated timestamp: Each segment table shows a small "Last forecast" or "Data through" date at the bottom right, so you can tell at a glance how fresh the data is.
▲ Intermittent segment — last sale date, weeks since last sale, event count, and avg units per event
en-container-planning:
🚢
Menu › Container Planning

Container Planning

Register and manage inbound containers, SKU quantities, and packing list status.

▲ Container Planning overview — Summary KPI bar (collapsed), status & product filter pills, container list

Container Status Flow

Draft → Final List Sent → Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) → Complete

① Screen Layout & Filters

📊 Summary Bar

A collapsible KPI section above the container list. The header always shows total · active · completed container counts and this month's · next month's inbound count. Expand it to reveal 6 KPI cards: Total Containers, Active Containers, Arriving This Month, Arriving Next Month, Inbound Units, and Total CBM.

🔍 Filter Bar

Search box, status filter pills, and product filter pills. Status supports multi-select (multiple statuses active at once). Product is single-select: All / FM / CC / SC / No SKU.

📋 Container List

Split into Active and Completed tabs. Click any container to open the detail panel on the right. Use the bottom checkboxes to select multiple containers for Excel Export or Bulk Status Change.

📅 Timeline View

Click the Container Timeline → button at the top to navigate to the Timeline page — your current search query and filters are preserved.

ControlDescription
🔍 SearchReal-time filtering by container number, factory name, or SKU code.
Status filter pills
(multi-select)		 Draft  Final  Shipped  Complete — Toggle each pill on or off individually. Multiple statuses can be active at once. Selecting none shows all statuses.
Product filter pills
(single-select)		 All · FM (Floor Mat) · CC (Car Cover) · SC (Seat Cover) · No SKU. Selecting No SKU shows only containers that have no SKUs assigned yet.
Active / Completed tabsSwitch between in-progress containers (Draft · Final · Shipped) and completed containers. Selecting the Completed tab automatically activates only the Complete status filter.

② Creating a Container

1

Click + Add Container

Click the + Add Container button in the top-right of the screen.

2

Enter Basic Information

Enter the container number (e.g., 182-CA-SEAT), ETA, destination warehouse, and CBM capacity (default 80).

3

Add SKUs

Enter the Master SKU and register quantities. Bulk upload via Excel is also available (Import Excel).

4

Allocate Available Stock (optional)

To assign warehouse surplus to this container, use the Available Stock button.

5

Update Status

Change to Final List Sent once you send the order to the factory. Change to Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) when you receive the packing list.

▲ Add Container — New container registration form opened by clicking + Add Container

③ Editing a Container

Draft and Final List Sent containers can be freely edited or deleted — including basic details and the SKU list.

Editing Basic Information

FieldDescription
Container NumberClick directly in the Container Details section to edit.
Est. LoadingEstimated factory loading or shipment preparation date.
ETD NGBEstimated departure date from Ningbo (NGB) port.
ETA LAX/LGBEstimated arrival date at the LA/LB port.
Warehouse (ETA)Estimated date the container will be received at the destination warehouse.
Destination WarehouseSelect from the dropdown.
CBM CapacityEdit the total container capacity (default 80 m³).
FactoryType to select from already-registered factories in the combo input, or enter a new factory name. Saving with a new name automatically adds it to the factory database so it appears in the dropdown for future containers.
NotesEnter packing instructions or any other free-form notes.
🏭
Managing Factories: View, edit, or delete registered factories at Planning › Factories. Use that page to clean up any factory names added by mistake.

Adding SKUs

1

Enter Master SKU

Type the SKU code in the Master SKU search field at the bottom of the SKU Order Quantities section.

2

Enter Quantity

Fill in the order quantity in the Qty field. CBM/Unit is automatically looked up from the SKU master.

3

Click + Add

Click + Add to add the SKU to the list. To register multiple SKUs at once, use CSV/Excel Import.

SKU Memo

Each SKU row has a Memo field (last column in the SKU table). Use it to flag SKUs that should be removed first if the container's CBM exceeds capacity before the factory order is placed.

💡
Typical use: When planning a container near the CBM limit, mark lower-priority SKUs with a memo such as remove if over CBM. This makes it easy to identify which SKUs to cut when the factory confirms final quantities.

Memo values are preserved when re-importing via CSV/Excel — as long as the SKU code matches, the existing memo is kept.

CSV/Excel Import Behavior

⚠️
CSV/Excel Import replaces the entire SKU list.
The import uses overwrite mode: only SKUs present in the file are kept. Any SKUs currently in the container but not in the file will be removed.
CaseResult
SKU exists in both container and fileQty and CBM are updated to the file values. Existing Available Stock allocation and Memo are preserved.
SKU is in the file but not in the containerAdded as a new SKU.
SKU is in the container but not in the fileRemoved from the container.
💡
To add SKUs without removing existing ones, use the manual + Add entry instead of Import.

Deleting SKUs (Bulk Delete)

Available in Draft and Final List Sent containers. Select SKUs using the row checkboxes and click Delete Selected (N).

ItemDescription
Individual selectionCheck the checkbox on the left of each row.
Select all visibleHeader checkbox (Select visible SKUs) selects all SKUs matching the current search filter.
Delete restrictionSKUs with Available Stock allocated cannot be deleted — remove the allocation first.
Confirmation dialog"Delete N selected SKU(s)?" — click OK to proceed.
Status restrictionAvailable for Draft and Final List Sent. The button is hidden for Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) and above.
⚠️
SKUs with Available Stock (Remaining / Mistake) allocated must have their allocation removed first via + Add Available Stock before deletion.
▲ Container Edit opened after selecting a Draft/Final List Sent Seat Cover container from the left list

④ Status Details

StatusEditable FieldsShown in Demand PlanningNotes
Draft All fields editable/deletable ✅ Always shown Draft stage. Edit freely.
Final List Sent All fields editable/deletable ✅ Always shown After factory submission, it can still be edited like Draft: basic details, schedule, SKU add/delete, and quantity changes.
Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) Read-only ✅ Always shown After packing list received. No changes allowed.
Complete Read-only ❌ Not shown Received. Fully excluded from Demand Planning. Deletion requires Planner/Admin.
⚠️
Deleting a Container: Containers can be deleted while they are in Draft or Final List Sent status. Deleting an entire container requires Planner or Admin role. The delete option is available at the bottom of the container detail panel. Deleted containers and their SKU quantities cannot be recovered.

⑤ Complete Status and Demand Planning Display Rules

⚠️
Complete containers are not shown in Demand Planning.
The moment a container is changed to Complete, it immediately disappears from the Demand Planning container column list and is excluded from per-SKU Con. Qty calculations.

This behavior exists because inventory from a received container is already reflected in the warehouse's live stock figures (Current Stock). Subsequent order planning should be recalculated against the updated live stock numbers.

🚫 Conditions that Exclude from Demand Planning

  • Status = Complete
  • Container column disappears; Con. Qty excluded from calculations
  • Not reflected in Auto-Fill results (📅 / 📊)
  • Not shown in SKU Planning › Inventory & Inbound container list

✅ Conditions that Show in Demand Planning

  • Status = Final List Sent
  • Status = Shipped (Packing List Rcvd)
  • Status = Draft (only when Include Draft toggle is ON)

Demand Planning Changes When Status Transitions

Draft → Final List Sent → Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) → Complete ❌

The moment status changes to Complete, the container is removed from the Demand Planning container list.

SituationRecommended Action
Container has actually been receivedChange to Complete → Excluded from Demand Planning automatically. Verify updated stock in Demand Planning's Current Stock column.
Accidentally set to Complete before actual receiptFind the container in Container Planning and revert to Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) — it will immediately reappear in Demand Planning.
Need to review a Complete container's detailsView it directly in Container Planning. Complete containers are not shown in Demand Planning or SKU Planning.
✅
Correct Complete process:
  1. Confirm physical receipt, then click the container in Container Planning
  2. Change status to Complete
  3. After the external inventory DB syncs, click 🔄 Sync in Demand Planning
  4. Confirm the Current Stock figures have updated, then review the purchase plan
⚠️
CBM Overload Warning: Current CBM usage is shown at the top. Do not exceed 80 CBM.

⑥ Excel Export

Container Planning has two Export functions. Use each based on your purpose.

📊 Multi-Container Consolidated Export

Select multiple containers to compare and organize SKU quantities in one file. Used for Final Order compilation.

Button: Excel Export (N) in the top header

📋 Single Container Packing List Export

Export a single container's SKU list in packing list format.

Button: CSV/Excel at the bottom of the container detail panel

A. Multi-Container Consolidated Export (Excel Export)

Select multiple containers and export them as a single Excel file. Used to compare order quantities across containers or to deliver the Final Order to the factory.

1

Select Containers

Click the checkboxes on the left of the container list. Multiple selections allowed.

Use the Select visible checkbox to select/deselect all containers matching the current filter at once. Count shown as N selected.

2

Click Excel Export

Click the Excel Export (N) button (blue border). N is the number of selected containers. Disabled when 0 are selected.

3

File Downloads

The Excel file downloads automatically.

File Name Convention

{Product} ETA {M.DD.YYYY} ({First Container} - {Last Container} FINAL).xlsx
Example: Seat Cover ETA 8.15.2026 (SC-001 - SC-003 FINAL).xlsx
File Name ElementHow It Is Determined
ProductSingle product category → that product name. Mixed categories → "Mixed Product".
ETAThe earliest ETA among selected containers, formatted as M.DD.YYYY.
Container RangeFirst to last container number after sorting. Shown identically for a single selection.

Excel File Contents (Sheet: "Final Order")

ColumnContent
Product TypeShows "Custom" for Custom SKUs. Blank for standard SKUs.
Master SKUSKU code, sorted alphabetically.
[Container numbers]One column per selected container with the SKU's quantity. Shows 0 if not included.
Remaining StockSum of Available Stock Remaining quantity, fetched live at export time.
Total rowSum of quantities for each container column.
💡
Apply the Status / Product filter first, then use "Select visible" to quickly select containers of a specific product category or status.

B. Single Container Packing List Export (CSV/Excel)

Export an individual container's SKU list in packing list format. Used to share packing details with the factory or for internal review.

1

Open Container Detail

Click a container in the list to open the right detail panel.

2

Click CSV/Excel

Click CSV/Excel in the button area at the bottom of the detail panel.

This button is only shown for Draft and Final List Sent status. Hidden for Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) and above.

File Name Convention

container-{ContainerNumber}-{TodayDate}.xlsx
Example: container-SC-001-2026-06-15.xlsx

Excel File Contents (Sheet: "Packing List")

ItemContent
Header InfoContainer number, ETA, Factory, Destination, Notes, Status — output as 6 header rows
Master SKUSKU code
SourceQuantity origin. If allocated from Available Stock: "Remain {ref} (N)". If entered directly: "Container / Manual".
QtySKU quantity in this container
CBM / UnitCBM per unit for the SKU
Total CBMQty × CBM / Unit
Total rowTotal quantity and CBM sum

⑦ Bulk Status Change

Select multiple containers at once and change their status in a single action. Useful, for example, when several containers whose factory orders were just sent should all move to Final List Sent together.

1

Select Containers

Click the checkboxes on the left of the container list to select the containers whose status you want to change. This shares the same selection as Excel Export, so you can change status right after selecting containers for export.

Use the Select visible checkbox to select all containers matching the current filter at once.

2

Click Bulk Status

Click the Bulk Status (N) button in the top header. N is the number of currently selected containers.

The button is disabled when 0 containers are selected or when you lack container edit permission.

3

Review Current Status Distribution

The top of the modal shows how many of the selected containers currently fall into each of Draft · Final · Shipped · Complete.

4

Click the Target Status

Clicking a target status button applies it immediately, with no additional confirmation dialog. Each button shows how many containers will actually be changed (e.g., "3 to update"); if every selected container already has that status, the button shows "Already applied" and is disabled.

5

Check the Result

Once processing finishes, a toast message reports the success/failure counts (e.g., "Updated 3 container statuses."). Successfully updated containers are automatically deselected.

ItemDescription
Target filteringAmong the selected containers, any that already match the target status are automatically skipped — only containers with a different status are updated.
No order restrictionUnlike single-container status changes, there is no step-by-step "next only" flow or revert confirmation. You can jump directly to any of the 4 statuses (e.g., reverting several containers back to Draft in one action is allowed).
PermissionOnly users with container edit permission can use this feature. Without it, the button is disabled and a tooltip explains why.
Partial failure handlingContainers are processed one by one; if some fail (e.g., a network error) while others succeed, the successful changes are still applied, and success/failure counts are reported separately.
⚠️
Caution when bulk-changing to Complete: Setting multiple containers to Complete at once removes all of them from Demand Planning simultaneously. Make sure none of the selected containers are still awaiting actual receipt before proceeding. See ⑤ Complete Status and Demand Planning Display Rules for details.
en-container-timeline:
📅
Menu › Planning › Container Timeline

Container Timeline

Visualizes inbound containers by their display date (Confirmed Delivery date → ETA LAX/LGB → ETA, in that priority order) across three switchable views: Gantt (3M / 6M / All), Month Calendar, and Schedule List. Select the view from the dropdown in the toolbar, and click any container to open its SKU detail drawer.

▲ Container Timeline – Gantt view based on each container's display date. Status & product filters, period buttons, and search control what is displayed. Each month header shows the container count for that month.
📌
What is the "display date"? Every view (Gantt · Month Calendar · Schedule) picks a single date per container using this priority: ① Confirmed Delivery date (always used once set) → ② ETA LAX/LGB (estimated port arrival) → ③ ETA (estimated warehouse arrival). Confirmed containers show a ✓ mark with the confirmed date in chips and rows; unconfirmed containers show ETA LAX/LGB (or ETA if that's not set).

① Screen Layout

🔍 Filter Bar

Search box, status filter pills (multi-select), product filter pills, and period buttons sit at the top of the screen. Change any setting and the Gantt table updates immediately.

📊 Gantt Table

Grouped by month. Each month header displays the container count for that month as (N). Rows are color-coded by status.

📋 Detail Drawer

Click any Gantt bar or row to slide open a detail panel on the right. Shows the SKU list, CBM fill rate, and container metadata.

📋 Back to List

Click the ← List button at the top to return to Container Planning — your current search query and filters are preserved.

② Filters & Period

ControlDescription
🔍 SearchReal-time filtering by container number, factory name, or origin/destination warehouse.
Status filter pills
(multi-select)		 Draft  Final  Shipped  Complete Toggle each status on or off individually. Multiple statuses can be active simultaneously. Selecting none shows all statuses.
Product filter pills
(single-select)		 All · FM (Floor Mat) · CC (Car Cover) · SC (Seat Cover) · No SKU. Filter is preserved when navigating from Container Planning. Selecting No SKU shows only containers with no SKUs assigned.
View dropdown
(period / view selector)		 3 Months – Gantt showing only containers whose display date falls within the next 3 months. (Default)
6 Months – Gantt for containers whose display date falls within 6 months.
All – Gantt auto-calculates the full date range and shows every container.
Month View – Switches to a Google Calendar-style monthly calendar. Each date cell shows chips for containers whose display date falls on that day. Users with Container Timeline edit permission can drag a chip onto another date cell to move that container's date.
Schedule View – Switches to a date-grouped list view for the selected month. Containers are grouped by display date in chronological order.
💡
Want to focus on a specific status? Toggle the status pills to show only what you need — e.g., activate only Shipped to see containers in transit. Filter state is preserved when you navigate between Container Planning and Container Timeline.

③ Reading Gantt Bars

The Gantt table groups containers by month. Each month header shows the container count as (N) next to the month label. Each row represents one container.

ItemDescription
Bar color Draft red  |  Final List Sent amber  |  Shipped (Packing List Rcvd) blue  |  Complete green
Bar lengthRepresents the sea transit period (~30 days). The bar spans from the estimated departure date to the display date.
Bar positionThe right edge of the bar aligns with the display date (Confirmed Delivery → ETA LAX/LGB → ETA, in priority order) on the X-axis.
Left info areaShows container number, factory name, status badge, display date, SKU count, and a CBM fill-rate progress bar. Confirmed containers show (Confirmed - time) after the container number instead of a date.
ClickClick a row or Gantt bar to open the detail drawer on the right.

④ Month Calendar View

Select Month View from the view dropdown to switch to a Google Calendar-style monthly calendar. Each date cell displays up to four container chips whose display date (Confirmed Delivery → ETA LAX/LGB → ETA, in priority order) falls on that day. Overflow is collapsed into a +N more button. Users with Container Timeline edit permission can drag a chip onto another date cell to move that container's date immediately.

▲ Month Calendar View — display-date-based container chips appear in each date cell. The blue badge in the header shows the total container count for the month.
ItemDescription
Calendar headerThe blue badge next to the month name shows the total container count for that month. Use the ‹ / › buttons to navigate months.
Container chipsEach chip displays the container number and SKU count on a status-colored background. Confirmed containers show a ✓ mark before the container number. Up to four chips are shown per cell.
+N more buttonWhen more than four containers share the same display date, a +N more button appears. Hover over it to expand a popup showing all containers for that day.
Click a containerClick any chip or popup entry to open the detail drawer on the right.
Drag & drop to change date
(permission required)		Only users with Container Timeline edit permission (Role Permissions › Planning › Container Timeline › Edit) can drag chips. Drop a chip onto another date cell to change that container's date immediately — for confirmed containers this updates the Confirmed Delivery date; for all others it updates ETA LAX/LGB. The change is applied optimistically; if saving fails it reverts to the original date and shows an error. Users without this permission cannot drag chips — clicking still opens the detail drawer.
View persistenceThe selected view (Month View) is saved and restored automatically when you navigate away and return.

⑤ Schedule View

Select Schedule View from the dropdown to switch to a date-grouped list view for the selected month. Containers are grouped by their display date (Confirmed Delivery → ETA LAX/LGB → ETA, in priority order) in chronological order. Click any row to open the detail drawer. (Drag-and-drop date editing is only available in Month View.)

▲ Schedule View — containers grouped by display date in a chronological list. The blue badge in the header shows the total container count for the month.
ItemDescription
Month headerShows the total container count for the displayed month, ‹ / › navigation buttons, and a Today button to jump back to the current month.
Date groupsContainers are grouped by their display date, with each group header showing the date and day of week.

en-transit-stock:
🚢

Transit Stock

Record and track SKUs moving between warehouses, and reflect them in the Demand Planning grid.

The Transit Stock page is an operational log for inventory currently in transit between warehouses. The total quantity of all In Transit records for a given SKU is automatically reflected in the Transit Stock column of the Demand Planning grid.

ℹ️
Transit Stock & Demand Planning: Adding, deleting, or marking a record as Arrived immediately recalculates fc_stats.transit_stock and updates the Demand Planning grid. No manual Refresh required.

Add a Single Record

Click + Add Record in the top-right to open the form.

Source Warehouse *The warehouse the stock is departing from.
Destination Warehouse *The warehouse the stock is arriving at. Must differ from Source.
Master SKU *The SKU being moved. Automatically converted to uppercase on save.
Qty *Quantity in transit (integer, minimum 1).
NotesOptional. Container number, reason, or any free-text note.

The record is created with In Transit status and the SKU's Transit Stock is updated immediately.

Bulk Excel Import

Use this when you need to register multiple SKUs at once under the same source → destination warehouse pair.

  1. Click Import Excel → select source and destination warehouses, then proceed to the next step.
  2. Download the template or prepare a file with the columns below.
  3. Upload the file — a preview of up to 50 rows is shown. Confirm to import.
Master SKURequired. Accepted column name variants: sku, master.
QtyRequired (integer ≥ 1). Accepted variants: quantity, 수량.
NotesOptional. Accepted variants: note, memo, 비고, 메모.
⚠️
Maximum 2,000 rows per import. The server returns an error if this limit is exceeded.

Status Management & Tab Filters

The tabs at the top of the page filter records by status.

In Transit Currently moving. Only the quantity of In Transit records is summed into Transit Stock in Demand Planning.
Arrived Stock has been received at the destination. Excluded from the Transit Stock total; no further edits allowed.
Cancelled Movement was cancelled. Excluded from the Transit Stock total.

Action Buttons

✓ ArrivedOnly visible on In Transit rows. Marks the record as Arrived and immediately deducts it from Transit Stock.
DeleteAvailable on all records regardless of status. Transit Stock is recalculated after deletion.
